title: C Values, Integer Conversions, and Size Arithmetic · Spiral Systems Review
summary: After a delay, a release reviewer reconstructs the checked size and conversion policy contract from partial traces, compares two near-miss implementations, and retrieves prior bounds and lifetime rules before feedback. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for checked size and conversion policy after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Select integer types from range, signedness, rank, width, and representation requirements rather than familiar spelling.
Trace integer promotions and usual arithmetic conversions before comparing, shifting, indexing, or sizing.
Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
